perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Confidential Information" means all non-public information of CMHC and the Absence Management Services, and all personal information of CMHC employees, disclosed to the Proponent in aggregate or otherwise, including, without limitation, any functional, technical, operational and business information re lating to CMHC or its Absence Management Program including, but not limited to, demographics, policies, procedures, absence management details, financial data, product/service specifications and designs, data models, member data, medical data, return to work plans and communication plans. It also includes information orally disclosed if the disclosing party indicates at the time of disclosure the confidential or proprietary nature of the information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Confidential Information" means all non-public information of CMHC and the Absence Management Services, and all personal information of CMHC employees, disclosed to the Proponent in aggregate or otherwise, including, without limitation, any functional, technical, operational and business information re lating to CMHC or its Absence Management Program including, but not limited to, demographics, policies, procedures, absence management details, financial data, product/service specifications and designs, data models, member data, medical data, return to work plans and communication plans. It also includes information orally disclosed regardless of whether the disclosing party indicates at the time of disclosure the confidential or proprietary nature of the information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for the disclosing party to indicate the 'confidential or proprietary nature' of orally disclosed information for it to be classified as 'Confidential Information,' this change introduces contradiction. The original text implies that orally disclosed information is confidential only if explicitly indicated as such. The modified text now states it includes information orally disclosed regardless of whether the disclosing party indicates. This creates a contradiction concerning what constitutes 'Confidential Information' and therefore conflicts with section 2.1 which discusses how the Proponent should treat 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE 2: R ESTRICTIONS ON USE AND DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Confidentiality. The Proponent shall hold the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence, and shall not disclose, directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information to any person or entity outside of the Proponent without the prior written consent of CMHC. The Proponent shall employ such precautions as are necessary to prevent unauthorized use, access to and disclosure of Confidential Information, including but not limited to, ensuring that Confidential Information is disclosed only to those of its and its affiliates’ officers and employees who: (a) have a need to know the same; (b) have been advised of the proprietary and confidential nature of the information, and the confidentiality obligations set out in this Agreement; and (c) who are bound by a similar duty of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that orally disclosed information is considered 'Confidential Information' only if the disclosing party indicates its confidential nature at the time of disclosure. The changed text removes this requirement, making all orally disclosed information 'Confidential Information' regardless of any indication. This directly contradicts section 2.1, which obligates the Proponent to protect 'Confidential Information,' because under the changed definition, the Proponent is now responsible for protecting orally disclosed information even if it was never indicated as confidential, creating an inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Return of Documents. The Proponent agrees that all originals, copies, records, notes and summaries (paper or electronic) of any nature provided by CMHC, including the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be surrendered or, at CMHC’s option, certified destroyed, to CMHC immediately upon request.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Return of Documents. The Proponent agrees that all originals, copies, records, notes and summaries (paper or electronic) of any nature provided by CMHC, including the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, can be retained by the Proponent without any obligation of been surrendered or destroyed, to CMHC immediately upon request.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause regarding the return or destruction of documents containing Confidential Information. The original clause requires the Proponent to surrender or destroy these documents upon request, while the modified clause states that the documents can be retained by the Proponent without any obligation of been surrendered or destroyed. This is a blatant contradiction.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE 2: R ESTRICTIONS ON USE AND DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE 2: R ESTRICTIONS ON USE AND DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 Return of Documents. The Proponent agrees that all originals, copies, records, notes and summaries (paper or electronic) of any nature provided by CMHC, including the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, can be retained by the Proponent without any obligation of been surrendered or destroyed, to CMHC immediately upon request.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Proponent *agrees* to surrender or destroy the documents, while the changed text says the Proponent *can* retain the documents without any obligation to surrender or destroy them. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text and therefore this creates a direct contradiction because it removes the previously agreed upon obligation. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 4.8 Continuing Obligations. The Confidential Information provided to the Proponent shall retain its confidential nature and the requirements of use and confidentiality shall survive termination of this Agreement and the return of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.8 Continuing Obligations. The Confidential Information provided to the Proponent shall not retain its confidential nature after the termination of this Agreement and requirements of use and confidentiality shall not survive termination of this Agreement and the return of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause 4.8 explicitly states that the confidentiality obligations survive the termination of the agreement. By negating this statement, the modified clause directly contradicts it, now implying that all confidentiality requirements cease upon termination, no longer retains its confidential nature. This in-text contradiction creates significant uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.8 Continuing Obligations. The Confidential Information provided to the Proponent shall not retain its confidential nature after the termination of this Agreement and requirements of use and confidentiality shall not survive termination of this Agreement and the return of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidentiality obligations survive termination, while the changed text explicitly states that they do not. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, meaning that the change introduces a direct contradiction. Because the contradicted text is identical to the changed text, this means there is no survival of the confidentiality agreement at one point and a survival of the confidentiality agreement at another, leading to an internal contradiction as defined by 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 The Proponent agrees that a breach by it of any term or condition of this Agreement would result in serious and irreparable harm to CMHC which could not be completely compensated by monetary damages. The Proponent expressly agrees that CMHC shall be entitled to secure an appropriate legal remedy, including injunction or declaratory judgment, in the event of a breach or threatened breach of any term of this Agreement to enable CMHC to protect its rights hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 The Proponent agrees that a breach by it of any term or condition of this Agreement would not result in serious and irreparable harm to CMHC which could not be completely compensated by monetary damages. The Proponent expressly agrees that CMHC shall not be entitled to secure an appropriate legal remedy, including injunction or declaratory judgment, in the event of a breach or threatened breach of any term of this Agreement to enable CMHC to protect its rights hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By negating the first sentence of section 3.1, the clause now contradicts itself and the general intent of the agreement. The original text asserts that breaches would cause irreparable harm and entitles CMHC to legal remedies. The edited text asserts the opposite of the previous statement, contradicting it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 The Proponent agrees that a breach by it of any term or condition of this Agreement would not result in serious and irreparable harm to CMHC which could not be completely compensated by monetary damages. The Proponent expressly agrees that CMHC shall not be entitled to secure an appropriate legal remedy, including injunction or declaratory judgment, in the event of a breach or threatened breach of any term of this Agreement to enable CMHC to protect its rights hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 3.1 directly contradicts itself and the contradicted text, which is in the same location. The original text states that a breach would cause irreparable harm, entitling CMHC to legal remedies. The changed text states the opposite, claiming that breaches would not cause irreparable harm and that CMHC is not entitled to legal remedies. This creates a clear and direct conflict, falling under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the altered text now has conflicting implications compared to its original intent.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Amendment. Except as otherwise provided herein, this Agreement shall not be amended or modified, nor shall any waiver of any right hereunder be effective, unless set forth in a document executed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Amendment. Except as otherwise provided herein, this Agreement can be amended or modified verbally, nor shall any waiver of any right hereunder be effective, unless set forth in a document executed by only one of the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 4.2 clearly states that all amendments must be in writing and executed by *both* parties. By changing this agreement can be modified verbally and only one of the parties are needed for signature. This directly contradicts with the original statement, creating uncertainty around the modification of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Amendment. Except as otherwise provided herein, this Agreement can be amended or modified verbally, nor shall any waiver of any right hereunder be effective, unless set forth in a document executed by only one of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 4.2 states that the agreement can be modified verbally and executed by only one party. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, reinforces this. This directly contradicts the original text of section 4.2, which required amendments to be in writing and executed by both parties, creating a clear and direct conflict regarding the modification process. This falls under the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition because the clause has conflicting implications with its original form, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: 4.9 Assignment . This Agreement may not be assigned or transferred in whole or in part by the Proponent without CMHC’s prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.9 Assignment . This Agreement may be assigned or transferred in whole or in part by the Proponent without CMHC’s prior written consent, and CMHC's written consent is not required.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 4.9 clearly states that there may be no transferring of the agreement without CMHC written concent. By adding that the CMHC's concent is not required creates an in-text contraditction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.9 Assignment . This Agreement may be assigned or transferred in whole or in part by the Proponent without CMHC’s prior written consent, and CMHC's written consent is not required.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "and CMHC's written consent is not required" to the original text. The original text states that the agreement may not be transferred without CMHC's prior written consent. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that CMHC's consent is not required. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding whether CMHC's consent is needed for the assignment or transfer of the agreement.
